assumptions.distance: Miles in UI, kilometres inside energy and emissions calculations
assumptions.note: Seed data is intentionally transparent and replaceable. It is designed for portfolio engineering, not vehicle purchase advice.

rag_corpus.title: Project overview and architecture
rag_corpus.content: EV vs ICE Intelligence Lab is a portfolio-grade data product. It combines a Python data pipeline, Pandas data processing, NumPy and SciPy calculations, scikit-learn modelling, SQLite output, Next.js REST API routes, React dashboard components, SEO metadata, and GitHub Actions CI/CD.

rag_corpus.title: Data assumptions and limitations
rag_corpus.content: The seed dataset is a transparent UK demo dataset for engineering demonstration. It models ownership cost, energy cost, maintenance, depreciation, manufacturing emissions, use-phase emissions, grid carbon intensity, and driving mix. It is not vehicle purchase advice.

rag_corpus.title: Tesla Model 3 RWD 2024
rag_corpus.content: Tesla Model 3 RWD is a UK market 2024 compact-executive EV trim using electricity. Purchase price is GBP 39990. Efficiency is 14.4 kwh_per_100km. Manufacturing emissions are 9800 kgCO2e and annual maintenance is GBP 360.

rag_corpus.title: Scenario: City commuter
rag_corpus.content: The City commuter scenario uses 8000 annual miles over 4 years with 68 percent urban driving and 14 percent motorway driving. Grid intensity is 95.0 gCO2e/kWh. Weighted electricity price is GBP 0.28 per kWh. The lowest-cost trim is Dacia Sandero TCe 90 Essential at GBP 0.405 per mile. The lowest lifecycle emissions trim is Fiat 500e La Prima 42kWh at 8.25 tonnes CO2e.

rag_corpus.title: Scenario: Mixed household

The lowest lifecycle emissions trim is Fiat 500e La Prima 42kWh at 8.88 tonnes CO2e.

rag_corpus.title: Scottish Power EV Optimise
rag_corpus.content: Scottish Power EV Optimise is a UK smart_charging_add_on tariff. The default off-peak rate is 9.0 p/kWh with no separate peak EV rate because this is an add-on. The off-peak window is Anytime to Anytime for 24 hours. Standing charge is 0.0 p/day with scope: Add-on has no separate standing charge; existing household tariff still applies. Source is MoneySavingExpert dated 2026-04-30. Existing customers only; add-on EV charging rate applies to compatible smart charging rather than the whole home.

rag_corpus.title: EDF Energy GoElectric 12m with smart charging bolt-on
rag_corpus.content: EDF Energy GoElectric 12m with smart charging bolt-on is a UK time_of_use tariff. The default off-peak rate is 6.99 p/kWh and peak rate 29.75 p/kWh. The off-peak window is 23:00 to 06:00 for 7 hours. Standing charge is 57.21 p/day with scope: MSE says peak and standing charges are similar to the April Price Cap; region-specific standing charge should be checked with supplier. Source is MoneySavingExpert dated 2026-04-30. Electricity standing charge average 57.21p/day for 1 Apr to 30 Jun 2026 MSE notes 6.49p/kWh is available with a qualifying Pod Point charger and EDF smart charging terms.

Source is MoneySavingExpert dated 2026-04-30. Transcript also cites OVO Charge Anytime at about 14p/kWh Existing OVO customers only; smart charging add-on for compatible EVs and chargers.

rag_corpus.title: Signal processing over driving cycles
rag_corpus.content: SciPy signal processing smooths speed traces and extracts acceleration, deceleration, jerk RMS, stop share, and energy stress features. mixed has energy stress 71.1 and stop share 15.0 percent; motorway has energy stress 91.1 and stop share 20.0 percent; urban has energy stress 51.0 and stop share 35.0 percent.

rag_corpus.title: Machine learning cost model
rag_corpus.content: The ML model predicts total cost per mile using a scikit-learn RandomForestRegressor. It trains on 41400 synthetic scenario rows and evaluates on 9936 test rows. R2 is 0.999 and MAE is GBP 0.004 per mile. Top features are annual_miles (0.6511), purchase_price_gbp (0.2477), ownership_years (0.0732), annual_maintenance_gbp (0.0165), weighted_electricity_gbp_per_kwh (0.0026), efficiency_value (0.0023).

rag_corpus.title: Agentic RAG advisor design
rag_corpus.content: The Agentic RAG Advisor classifies a user query, chooses a scenario, retrieves relevant corpus documents, runs deterministic vehicle comparison calculations, selects a recommendation, and returns reasoning steps with citations. The design is local and explainable, with a clear upgrade path to vector search and LLM-generated reports.

source_registry.conflict_policy: Keep supplier/table/user-entered values side by side with source dates.
Use as an average benchmark only; supplier and region-specific charges should remain visible.

source_registry.refresh_mode: server route fetches the latest GOV.UK CSV
source_registry.conflict_policy: Return fetched values with source URL and fallback values when live refresh fails.
Summarise source values and keep them as secondary notes rather than overwriting current tariff references.
Keep all source values and label conflicts rather than overwriting.
Compare overlapping values with the selected catalog trim and return both values.

source_registry.source_name: DVSA MOT history and anonymised test data
source_registry.conflict_policy: Use as supporting evidence for usage and model text, not as trim authority.
Compare CO2 values by source and preserve disagreements.

api_examples: /api/rag?q=Which vehicle is best for high mileage emissions?
/api/agent?q=I drive 22000 miles a year and want low running costs
